subject: Public Health
title: Recovery and Hope
description: Learn Recovery and Hope in one minute.

hook.text: Millions of people have recovered from addiction—recovery is not only possible, it's common.

buildup.text: Recovery is a process, not an event—it involves rebuilding relationships, health, purpose, and community connections.

discovery.text: Peer support—people in recovery helping others—is one of the most powerful tools, from AA to recovery coaches.

twist.text: Relapse occurs in 40-60% of cases—similar to diabetes and hypertension. It's part of a chronic disease, not a failure.

climax.text: Recovery capital—housing, employment, social networks, and purpose—predicts success better than any single treatment.

punchline.text: Addiction can be the end of a story. But for millions, it's the middle.

quiz.question: How common is relapse in addiction recovery?
options: 40-60% of people relapse, similar to other chronic diseases
